title: lathyrus vernus var. alatus
intro: Lathyrus vernus var. alatus, also known as the woodland pea or alate wood vetch, is a variant of the Lathyrus vernus plant, a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a perennial legume with medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Eastern European medicine, where it is valued for its antispasmodic and diuretic effects. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including urinary problems, digestive issues, and respiratory infections, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving circulation. Herbal preparations of Lathyrus vernus var. alatus include tea and tincture, which can be used to make infusions for medicinal purposes.
